name: follow-the-3-step.spec.ts
description:
Follow the 3-step sessions in HOW_TO_TEST_STUDIO.md:
SESSION 1: Generate 2 easy tests (Google, Wikipedia)
SESSION 2: Tes

steps: Navigate to Google and perform a simple search
Navigate to Wikipedia and verify the homepage loads
Navigate to Stack Overflow and interact with a complex element to test healing
Execute an advanced test to evaluate the learning system's adaptation
selectors: Wikipedia homepage logo or main heading
Stack Overflow complex element (e.g., dynamic question list, login button)
Learning system UI components (e.g., adaptive element, recommendation panel)
risks: Dynamic element locators on Stack Overflow may change frequently
Google and Wikipedia may update their DOM structure, breaking selectors
Learning system UI may have non-deterministic elements or asynchronous rendering
Network latency or CAPTCHAs may cause test flakiness
healingStrategies: Use robust selectors with fallback strategies (e.g., text, role, data attributes)
Implement auto-healing with selector prioritization and retry logic
Leverage Playwright's locator chaining and strict mode for dynamic elements
Capture and log selector failures for learning system feedback and continuous improvement
